Fellow American,

**We're doing something BIG.**

We're hosting an amazing contest, and one lucky winner from Team Herschel will win a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.

On March 2, President Donald J. Trump and I are hosting the first annual "Great American Invitational" golf tournament in Palm Beach, and we're giving away one spot to a lucky winner!

In addition to winning a round of golf at President Trump's amazing International Golf Club, our winner will also win the following:
* Photo with President Trump and me.
* Admission to the awards luncheon.
* A commemorative hat signed by President Trump and me.
* Limited edition tournament shirt.
* Roundtrip airfare, ground transportation, and accommodations for one night

subject: End the California School Mask Mandate

body:
It’s time for California to join the rest of America and unmask our kids. Did you see what I saw at the Super Bowl, Peter? Besides a HUGE Rams win—70,000+ people, including numerous celebrities and politicians, enjoying the game maskless. But the next morning, I had to put masks on my 4- and 6-year-old kids when I dropped them off at school. [**SIGN THE PETITION:**
**UNMASK OUR KIDS**](https://link.mattjacobsforcongress.com/u/click?_t=efc64eece5bd468cad54456b97abcf7f&_m=462ba23acbe04cfaae50097148e3be83&_e=bNHMpANJ7mHxozBC8JJWlxB2N-II8HVWC5cAc6DcRfZfs6JoeF1km8tj4peKLdd5ybniWkvdLqyb2E108zssAp4Xm1ak3HOMBdoVmXV9gn7797tPIVYWlxvckPaKpM2uDv9VTUjl2z1isVjp1eGepllrP1m9NHriZDRUzUzS_TDpOAbpg3L6n4fNSkRtIKEhPVeYotPxkd9_69vrQmt-Zlpt4LpJxV6UUC4BtfgZLEt9tdulWww5ranFMiTG6MW1rwvZZL8BPhQgdGRZ9hyqkQlMCZfcGsFG9Sqm178Gtj_nV94325UzyXfm79uYAu0EaN0M2aJv7zrCYlJbWnPXSfL85i2VkVSg9fhI8I78VnOznF7b6MMV5dVZljWnSh3y_pLJPhhba7OzbzhNNfP4ETm9i1W6KZ_eeFK_KT04Z0hzzRZFmK6KtUJpob9aR0cli8LdKMhUvqE4zl_kwWG4XA%3D%3D) By now, the vast majority of states in America – including most states run by Democrats – have announced the elimination of school mask mandates. And here in Ventura County, the Superintendent of Schools just stated that the school mask mandate should be lifted locally. [**Add your name to the petition if you think it’s time to END THE CALIFORNIA SCHOOL MASK MANDATE:**](https://link.mattjacobsforcongress.com/u/click?_t=efc64eece5bd468cad54456b97abcf7f&_m=462ba23acbe04cfaae50097148e3be83&_e=bNHMpANJ7mHxozBC8JJWlxB2N-II8HVWC5cAc6DcRfZfs6JoeF1km8tj4peKLdd5ybniWkvdLqyb2E108zssAp4Xm1ak3HOMBdoVmXV9gn7797tPIVYWlxvckPaKpM2uDv9VTUjl2z1isVjp1eGepllrP1m9NHriZDRUzUzS_TDpOAbpg3L6n4fNSkRtIKEhPVeYotPxkd9_69vrQmt-Zlpt4LpJxV6UUC4BtfgZLEt9tdulWww5ranFMiTG6MW1gF1A4q33y94T1THuUSfUXGXoXp-cO5dtH_tLloZq8L2RRLu4K-zBLIA4K14rnAiq2Ud3a2C-AQuz0wBn25I3BWKH5yi8LaPWxPGCt_1aP3NmZO95gGaEasunyV-tO5CZ3tdsUrXIlNByFgNULlsLok_QVlD46OQgkCzqOEKHGQSCvhoNt2439yXmGXaUVt-_xZrwOTho6sIUVane_MGWZw%3D%3D) [](https://link.mattjacobsforcongress.com/u/click?_t=efc64eece5bd468cad54456b97abcf7f&_m=462ba23acbe04cfaae50097148e3be83&_e=bNHMpANJ7mHxozBC8JJWlxB2N-II8HVWC5cAc6DcRfZfs6JoeF1km8tj4peKLdd5ybniWkvdLqyb2E108zssAp4Xm1ak3HOMBdoVmXV9gn7797tPIVYWlxvckPaKpM2uDv9VTUjl2z1isVjp1eGepllrP1m9NHriZDRUzUzS_TDpOAbpg3L6n4fNSkRtIKEhPVeYotPxkd9_69vrQmt-Zlpt4LpJxV6UUC4BtfgZLEt9tdulWww5ranFMiTG6MW19Y4TEe4_jnDkiFJFYo4BZwWjzgv_WeY-Etm0nNIXgiE55BgGb9VxFE84wY7rkfOFbEbmRKxseOpEC7Y7xOopXRI_R4-OW6t8xwXbyu7RowXIMo_FbMeZ2YjM6mxB9P2G3-htzbRSQBR3fZXu26Z2Sk5bmchczrscrGRjnP-b2Nved1MYZ-2UAa4gjUPTnUoMviAtO93aIbrl1SGpX6HQYw%3D%3D) The widespread elimination of school mask mandates reflects the scientific consensus that the costs of masking schoolchildren – including learning loss, speech delays, social isolation, increased mental health problems, and heightened rates of suicidal ideation – outweigh any benefits. Despite the science, Governor Newsom and my opponent, Congresswoman Julia Brownley, continue to support the compulsory masking of all California schoolchildren. It’s time for Rep. A Tennessee doctor is being [accused](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=792dcf6813ca4da28611a94b2ce6e197&_e=g3UU7SzQxcDqsNqcM9sc6p5KvXY4ms5eJLo8M88H2PQBPdo4sWrsJ_k5APaHzKHizja-5kAG-CrXRU_pU2wxBuKuc8VsgIeWHmFnIPwABIqP4VdFMhi9TewjldEipDA6iP7BlWm7sMqcWSJKFM6o_YtDE0A9P1oE6xq5WHy5s6s5vZojFBJZEjT0xrgjQQAtvK0kSUeFC2NnggMnseTAlCcrJvyt4VEbclUStY467HrquyM-FACwfSmo4FcqOTpeB7ZHLYdGt2g8ZIXngT8NMaPkeUvqD-9ntWy8tBmf7C2NIrL4_3NWGZUET9vP-2sAtIVHhPYSSJrVMOf4XgNcWdk0jp1qWY52eVU5AquJ_YmegBrc3qdxrH8yZzJjQ-C1J29rkzktdAQNOfCCI3XFiw9pUuQHCcXMbXMVyQEbKgwTWfaek5x5BmT_KkxL-3-N5xOxNIJtGQ7Vce5_FCmJe8P7sJMfIgpLbD8MLGuvU-w%3D) of distributing nearly 5 million addictive painkiller pills over eight years, serving as the primary supplier for three drug trafficking organizations. That’s four times the national average. The worst part? Normally, Dr. David Bruce Coffey’s crimes would get at least [20 years in prison](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=792dcf6813ca4da28611a94b2ce6e197&_e=g3UU7SzQxcDqsNqcM9sc6p5KvXY4ms5eJLo8M88H2PQBPdo4sWrsJ_k5APaHzKHi2FGRYVwLACUsKSAOIPNgu0NdCpTzoe_6WanTdLkCTV9nCTfg1RQL2YYDVVc8maQY3VD_iYznW_5tOKt-94eBKkKSmQF6SqVxcVUaY2_40B_RC_7TQ7EQyNZ5EBCW5pfv8eghYqtzGPZI9rsv4reqNiYTBpiLvWljh2aFJCkq3EUjJFAmOoqQ6KJP38-bJ1xQEKpJTDF23IUW7ak1_JrH-shDOSkbgE4qpLavbLG81s4DPGTVp0xQJDKR58Cqnu7Vq5DJxF3yohrw2NmKI2rcocm6vX_-UD9onDK6LF6znfmpGIx69E8Sw720SSjz4bVVsFoFigtFwehB45QJ0F-ZQThXeMoH8hkwjlJ9Oikzud2L7XBMOhpzDIar__NKRtIMRbpgsabYW2uCgjg1aPBW7w%3D%3D), but thanks to a plea deal in which he admitted to distributing only 60 pills, he probably won’t get more than a year.

**We have a serious problem:** In 2020 Tennessee [recorded](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=792dcf6813ca4da28611a94b2ce6e197&_e=g3UU7SzQxcDqsNqcM9sc6izwY3nNGQB0NiBdNt8tH79PotM8JVym1DxpR8DWAdkHzPJ0fOcbXTLm6Nh1nOFADcMxAzIZMX-12j-YptKqVhab3wVKQuwoOYDHxhvPCmd1levdo279qId0KLu7r1OC8mM4sDwU0lNixrKFL2lugA_xp1sP21yqkMcT3ilPLeoveu3Ir0RJG88idYExYWZUM60MjNThwozbwwU8SXc_BVnAlhRc9oUFUZ5odJQsOmZ4jVvxxlstfTehiX1DClv-X_CJun9tXH-AwwZuiog-la9nsJz4fiTUBZOzPCOmUxhGEcuJXtKhkC-4eW_xLe3sSimsTBTcfypULss7p1zBK_zDU9GbXsIlfpiDU0wjYcIOTYLAV9KhEHwrrAkwkC9NkzsGUWfHeoQguEOnyjeSCzf1bHVtZaKCJPddAC7tPJ_AL0SmdJPQPhORWcMPu5MOUg%3D%3D) 3,032 overdose deaths, a 45% increase from 2019. Opioids were involved in 79% of these cases.

Let me be blunt: Dr. Coffey is scum of the earth, and our judicial system is letting him off the hook. Doctors take an oath. They are supposed to help people, but perpetrators like Dr. Coffey do the opposite. How many more people have to die and how many more lives have to be destroyed before we start taking the opioid epidemic seriously?

That's why I [introduced](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=792dcf6813ca4da28611a94b2ce6e197&_e=g3UU7SzQxcDqsNqcM9sc6rdDW2xRbteiWmyxwGZyxstnMC7ClJ5MrHwQ_tKhJm5FGnXkCdjGjMdSX4PZext76qUXtgtXznMi3w22TUG46RC_8mt4sy-0cFcdbAl-0LkV0tHlHLArh_sHp95Bvu4uH44EHE_93y1tTGceKJkzfNOl37O2cqWx0_60E_ozBmdIVogD6vLDhc-d0GXXG2gReTTSalsZrcrXZ6r73Ypz13m_x4ALGPhm87r1QTnenSO8xkdydruNA-UXEGes0kWYlBdNrsWDkbekAtsPYIVr30Mf0YEnvMMUU-GTV3ovAiGUb4UmGLTzp-7RE7_s0yS-Tw%3D%3D) the Fentanyl Trafficker Elimination Act, to address the huge amounts of fentanyl illegally trafficked into America. In 2021, U.S. Customs and Border Patrol agents [seized](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=792dcf6813ca4da28611a94b2ce6e197&_e=g3UU7SzQxcDqsNqcM9sc6pJZFa9WCh3IrIbzo1M10m06ibixx2Tnhygp2SKzqP1nG7hvqqXlJoErkQTHaHHb4Ss2HPSp76ReCsPGPa9grvxnNGGXuhWLsx58tZaUtck_9b53LDk7kMv3qjR3Uj38MipUx9yVN2Js-PpQIVPzb7FC2GKEAHudN2Zd-uwUCYd-3wv-3Tl6p6qlCKe3Wt7a1UpgCGuaycTdPreRC9_j0wdn14PhL4m2ETp7tSRpC4VmIoU3Hq6bagIKKB91eVLEdizRgGG_zZBhLWpwlIC8cKey1ecTQH633FN_xitN8LZShSgo8DpF5ghmsRE3dyd1ZFycpZR6BELq1CVZBi9gXsz4A-PqjLFXLpbd7pimIw4Uji74isv6Y1xN88JE2VIjftU_XKIfDyOFGLn9pa8iv60d_4uqZ8f6gfggLUQQOV6IEoHGNuQQygCx-V42hv4ALL4oCvYsJV5RsbEX4fP1tlY%3D) over 11,000 pounds of fentanyl coming across the southern border, a 42% increase over the previous year. My bill would put international fentanyl traffickers in prison for life—where they belong.

I also called on Congress to revisit the sentencing guidelines for dirtbags like Dr. Coffey who are basically killing thousands of people and pocketing the extra cash.

**Valentine’s Day Spent With Veterans 💓**
[](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=792dcf6813ca4da28611a94b2ce6e197&_e=g3UU7SzQxcDqsNqcM9sc6p5KvXY4ms5eJLo8M88H2PRChzSQsdXgcKXbJD3W6j5t3Rj1VrQXg7Q9QoloP_5ZGfk_LJvwbWUGFqDSrXm5llcS-URFH_LXhD9Bes6RAzn1raVMjAOR6SWSHPMZ5oJy7TT9fFPbKBH6dipPSO1wR8vSBGfGNk4Qe9KgaWQxNDFngCBiKJGZASieTOTRc1PaHbmpJ7ymRMY2mEy4sx0OfrB4r17QZClEw7MFSpJSKaMlQNbTAQzK35qhMkziK-bVLRrxvs0oNelJ8XuNajug6946Vjcw8HijlF56PRFwZMUR5GVd0Rq6nKyQjD6b66mvT3fszqF5r-7M7mOUVXQT2_NNeFRA7zEbODk5VS30wJaNa6Z1vJCxm64ti9x0FsURHLNEQ3nvV3KYYbi6SKGyyyw%3D)
Valentine’s Day is the perfect time to express our admiration and gratitude for the ones we love. This Monday, I had the honor of doing just that by visiting with our nation’s heroes at the Ben Atchley State Veterans Home.

Thanks to the Knox County Volunteer Republican Women’s Club’s generosity and mad art skills, I delivered their homemade valentines to some of East Tennessee’s best.

This visit was particularly special because my dad—a Marine who served in World War II—spent time at this same home towards the end of his life. I am so grateful for the veterans who dedicated their lives to protecting us and our freedoms and for the staff who take such good care of our heroes. Don’t tell my wife, but this was the best Valentine’s Day present a guy could ask for.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Mark=20Finchem?=
subject: The audit means something
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FINCHEM FOR SECRETARY OF STATE
body:
Time to execute |
---
| | [ ](https://votefinchem.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a653829701c13e4a704cb19bf&id=cb64a23a3f&e=44b8c466b0)
---
| | Dear ,
My name is Mark Finchem. I am the America First Conservative running for Secretary of State in Arizona – and the only candidate nationwide who has developed foolproof currency-grade watermarking for ballots, to make sure that they are secure.

Why do Liz Chaney and Nancy Pelosi hate me? Because I am an America First candidate who has a real solution to making it easy to vote but hard to cheat.

[**And right now we are entering into the main push of our campaign. Our primary is August 2nd, which means we have just about 6 months to make history.**](https://votefinchem.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a653829701c13e4a704cb19bf&id=8cb6de7e18&e=44b8c466b0)

[_To do this, I need your help. Please help my campaign with a donation today. It’s go time._](https://votefinchem.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a653829701c13e4a704cb19bf&id=89aeb5c177&e=44b8c466b0)

What the Maricopa Audit taught us is that process matters. Simplicity matters. Reform isn’t a move towards greater complexity. It is almost always reductive.

Take bloated systems and trim the fat. Take corrupt systems and stop the juice from flowing.

Take chaotic systems and make sure that they are trackable and auditable.

**Reforming our elections is simple. But not easy.**

**_Maricopa showed us that in at least several key counties and precincts in Arizona, the elections were irredeemably flawed._**

Now what?

I want to bring process, order, and transparency back to the office of Secretary of State and to our elections. Much ado has been made about the fact that what once was a largely bureaucratic office is getting so much scrutiny and attention this election cycle.

It doesn’t take a lot to see the answer.

**Bureaucratic jobs are simply bureaucratic when the bureaucracy functions. When the processes are flawed and the system if rife with corruption and fraud, the job becomes one of reform.**

And that is exactly what I will bring to Arizona. Period.
Paper ballots. Hand counting. Establish an Audit Trail.

**It’s that simple. But doing this requires fighting back against an establishment that wants to obfuscate the system in totality, making it impossible to track, impossible to have transparency.**

What the Maricopa Audit taught us is that process matters, and when a process is irredeemably flawed, the result is irredeemable fraud.

**How do you eat an elephant? One bite at a time.**

One of the most crucial things that I am bringing to the table is currency-grade protection and authentication for ballots.
---
| [ ](https://votefinchem.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a653829701c13e4a704cb19bf&id=4827a7c758&e=44b8c466b0)
---
| Right now, I am petitioning and doing everything in my power to have this adopted in all 50 states. We need ballots as safe and reliable as the US Dollar.

**Currency grade is the only way to make sure that Arizona becomes the Gold Standard for Election Integrity.**

But making this push requires huge amounts of money and we have very little time left. It’s already 2022!

[**Can you help me with a generous donation today?

Anything can help and will go a long way to bringing Election Integrity into reality!**](https://votefinchem.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a653829701c13e4a704cb19bf&id=eaaeb2bb05&e=44b8c466b0)

[Thank you for your support!](https://votefinchem.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a653829701c13e4a704cb19bf&id=4fd31aab17&e=44b8c466b0)
---
| [ ](https://votefinchem.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a653829701c13e4a704cb19bf&id=58b1ef71aa&e=44b8c466b0)
---
| Mark Finchem

Arizona House of Representatives – District 11
Republican for Arizona Secretary of State
Conservative
Election Integrity Patriot

P.S. [Please contribute today.](https://votefinchem.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a653829701c13e4a704cb19bf&id=60146a1ad0&e=44b8c466b0)

PAID FOR BY FINCHEM FOR SECRETARY OF STATE
---
| |
---
| _Copyright © 2022 Mark Finchem for Secretary of State, All rights reserved._

**Our mailing address is:**
Mark Finchem for Secretary of State15508 W Bell Rd.STE 101-423Surprise, AZ 85374
[Add us to your address book](https://votefinchem.us1.list-manage.com/vcard?u=a653829701c13e4a704cb19bf&id=7ec8888fbb)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://votefinchem.us1.list-manage.com/profile?u=a653829701c13e4a704cb19bf&id=7ec8888fbb&e=44b8c466b0&c=b98ab30055) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://votefinchem.us1.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=a653829701c13e4a704cb19bf&id=7ec8888fbb&e=44b8c466b0&c=b98ab30055).
---

subject: A HUGE pre-endorsement win and a special invitation
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KEVIN MULLIN FOR CONGRESS
body:
We have big news! This week, our campaign reached a huge milestone: We won the California Democratic Party (CDP) pre-endorsement recommendation vote | | | |
---
| | | Peter,

We have big news!

This week, our campaign reached a huge milestone: We won the California Democratic Party (CDP) pre-endorsement recommendation vote with a staggering 75%!

The 2022 state convention isn't until next month, but this is a necessary and giant first step — and a powerful show of organizational strength — in order for us to secure the backing of the CDP.

Our campaign has now been endorsed by over one hundred elected officials from across our district and across California. Congresswomen Jackie Speier and Anna Eshoo, Hillsborough Mayor Alvin Royse, Millbrae Councilmember Reuben Holober, and the United Food and Commercial Workers Local 5 are some of the latest local backers to throw their support behind us.

[The #mullmentum keeps rolling, but Peter, can you rush a donation today to keep it going](https://kevinmullinforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=31b479dbea5973b27a9d19670&id=736734cb0c&e=292fde286a)[ ?](https://kevinmullinforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=31b479dbea5973b27a9d19670&id=1655f31b82&e=292fde286a)

And just a reminder about our event tomorrow morning in San Francisco with San Francisco Supervisors Ahsha Safai and Connie Chan - we’ll be talking to our neighbors in the Excelsior about why they should climb aboard our campaign. We’d love to have you there!

You can email [brian@kevinmullinforcongress.com](mailto:brian@kevinmullinforcongress.com) to get more information and sign up to attend.

Thank you for your continued support of our campaign. And let’s keep the #mullmentum rolling! **Team Mullin**
---

subject: Benson Campaign Update 2.22.21 📝
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MICHELLE FOR MINNESOTA COMMITTEE
body:
Hello friend, here is what we've been up to the past week on the campaign trail! | | | |
---
| | |
---
| Dear Fellow Republicans, This week has been so energizing on the campaign trail meeting with folks in corners of our state. It’s clear to me: Republicans are ready for a more prosperous Minnesota. I am the most principled, prepared, and **electable candidate and am ready to serve on Day 1.**
**CONDOLENCES TO THE FAMILY OF CONGRESSMAN HAGEDORN**
I am saddened at the passing of Congressman Hagedorn. Even as he battled cancer, he served the people of the First Congressional District with purpose. He was gracious in our meetings and always more interested in others than himself. My deepest condolences to his widow Jennifer, his family, and all who knew and loved him.

**DELEGATE****OUTREACH AND CONVENTIONS**
The last few weeks have been full of balancing my commitment as a State Senator and meeting with so many of our great Republican grassroots leaders. If we haven't connected by phone or in person yet, I look forward to very soon! This weekend our campaign will be at each BPOU convention and I hope to see you there.
---
| **MEETING WITH COLLEGE REPUBLICANS** This week I stopped by to chat with the College Republicans at the University of St. Thomas. It was exciting to be back on a campus I had spent time while a student at St. Kate's. These students are fired up to join the cause to elect a Republican for the first time in their lives!
---
|
---
---
| **VISITING MAPLE GROVE** This Thursday I visited with **Republicans in Maple Grove.** I also had a great time meeting with everyone who is concerned about the direction Tim Walz is taking us. I look forward to seeing so many of you on trail.

---
|
---
| **PARENTS' BILL OF RIGHTS** It’s simple. Schools should provide a syllabus to every class. [Y](https://michelle4mn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0e599d2cf739987622330d245&id=bf42816b11&e=9d6926af69)[ou can read more about my plan to get parents back in the classroom and put kids first.](https://michelle4mn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0e599d2cf739987622330d245&id=68534f74d6&e=9d6926af69)

---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| [DONATE](https://michelle4mn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0e599d2cf739987622330d245&id=1ae06e694f&e=9d6926af69 "DONATE")
---

---

name: OUST DEJOY, SAVE THE USPS >> (protectvoting.org)
subject: ADD YOUR NAME: Tell Biden to FIRE Louis DeJoy!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
SIGN NOW!
| TEXT JOIN TO 50224 TO GET IMPORTANT ACTION ALERTS FROM VOTER PROTECTION PROJECT! [ ](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/128?t=1&akid=1295%2E878179%2E5ilJ0T)
---
**_Team, the Postal Service is at a BREAKING POINT:_** A new report EXPOSED Trump crony Louis DeJoy’s awful plan to destroy the Postal Service – **_making mail delays even worse and prices more expensive._** | **The Postal Service is on the brink of collapse, and it’s all LOUIS DEJOY’S FAULT.** | [35,000 SIGNATURES NEEDED: TELL BIDEN TO OUST TRUMP’S POSTMASTER, LOUIS DEJOY, AND SAVE THE USPS »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/Oust-DeJoy/?source=VPP_20220218_EMPET_E3_T0_AC&t=7&akid=1295%2E878179%2E5ilJ0T)
---
Team, the USPS is one of America’s most vital and treasured institutions.

Americans across the country depend on the USPS for life-saving medications, documents, COVID tests, voting by mail, and more. We **_REFUSE_** to sit back and allow DeJoy to cut off this crucial lifeline for countless Americans!

But if President Biden doesn’t act soon, the consequences will be **disastrous.**

name: Tom Fitton
subject: Hillarygate: Did Clinton Spy on Trump?
body:
Judicial Watch VICTORY for Clean Elections!

[INSIDE JW]

Judicial Watch Victory: North Carolina Settles Voter Roll Lawsuit
after State Removes over 430,000 Inactive Names from Rolls

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26818&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dS5iZS9XOXR6WUh4bGlRUT91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
In a big win for us and voters in North Carolina, we settled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26804&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtbmMtZXQtYWwtbnZyYS1zZXR0bGVtZW50LTIxMS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
our lawsuit against the state and two of its counties after they
removed over 430,000 ineligible names from the voter rolls.

We filed the lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26805&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanVkaWNpYWwtd2F0Y2gtc3Vlcy10by1mb3JjZS1ub3J0aC1jYXJvbGluYS10by1jbGVhbi1pdHMtdm90ZXItcm9sbHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
against the state, Mecklenburg County and Guilford County in April
2020 (_Judicial Watch v. North Carolina and North Carolina State Board
of Elections, et al. _
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26806&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtbm9ydGgtY2Fyb2xpbmEtbnZyYS1jb21wbGFpbnQtMjExL10oTm8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=.
3:20-cv- 211)).

In June 2019, the U.S. Election Assistance Commission (EAC) had
released data
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=18459&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZWFjLmdvdi9yZXNlYXJjaC1hbmQtZGF0YS9kYXRhc2V0cy1jb2RlYm9va3MtYW5kLXN1cnZleXM/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
showing that voter registration rates in a significant proportion of
North Carolina’s 100 counties were close to, at or above 100% of
their age-eligible citizenry – statistics considered by the courts
to be a strong indication that a jurisdiction is not taking the steps
required by law to remove ineligible registrants. Our analysis also
showed that at the time of the EAC report the entire State of North
Carolina had a registration rate close to 100% of its age-eligible
citizenry.

On December 11, 2019, we sent notice-of-violation letters
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=17107&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL252cmEtbm90aWNlcy0yMDE5LW5vcnRoLWNhcm9saW5hLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
to the state and its two counties, warning them that lawsuits would be
filed if timely action were not taken to clean up the voter rolls.

In our April 2020 complaint
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=18458&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtbm9ydGgtY2Fyb2xpbmEtbnZyYS1jb21wbGFpbnQtMjExLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==],
we argued that North Carolina, Mecklenburg County, and Guilford County
failed to make reasonable efforts to remove ineligible voters from
their registration rolls as required by the federal National Voter
Registration Act of 1993 (NVRA). The lawsuit also claimed that these
jurisdictions violated the NVRA by failing to make available to us
public records concerning efforts to comply with the law.

On August 16, 2021, the EAC released its latest survey data
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26807&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZWFjLmdvdi9zaXRlcy9kZWZhdWx0L2ZpbGVzL2RvY3VtZW50X2xpYnJhcnkvZmlsZXMvMjAyMF9FQVZTX1JlcG9ydF9GaW5hbF81MDhjLnBkZj91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
from the states.

In the settlement, we told the court:

[T]he total number of inactive registrations reported by North
Carolina dropped from about 1.2 million in 2019, to about 765,000 in
2021 (a 36% drop). The statewide percentage of inactive registrations
dropped from 17% in 2019, which the complaint alleged to be a national
outlier, to 10% in 2021, which is close to the median state inactive
rate. The number of registrations removed for failure to respond to an
address confirmation notice and vote in two consecutive elections has
increased, from about 220,000 for the period reported in 2019, to
about 590,000 for the period reported in 2021 (a 168% increase).
With respect to the two North Carolina counties, we told the court:

Data for Mecklenburg County and Guilford County also showed
improvement. From 2019 to 2021, the percentage of inactive
registrations reported in Mecklenburg County dropped from 15.5% to
13%, and in Guilford County from 19% to 11%. The number of
registrations removed for failure to respond to an address
confirmation notice and vote in two elections increased during that
same period, from roughly 21,000 to 51,000 in Mecklenburg County, and
from 7,000 to 33,000 in Guilford County (a 142% and 372% increase,
respectively). In light of Defendants’ substantial increases in
removals of ineligible voters since this suit commenced, Plaintiff has
determined in good faith that this legal action should not be pursued.

In 2020, we also sued Pennsylvania
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=18726&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcHJlc3MtcmVsZWFzZXMvanctc3Vlcy1wYS12b3RpbmcvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz],
and
Colorado
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=20868&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcHJlc3MtcmVsZWFzZXMvY28tdm90ZXItcm9sbHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
for
failing to clean their voter rolls.

This is a win for the voters of North Carolina – because clean voter
rolls help pave the way to cleaner elections. After we filed our
federal lawsuit, North Carolina removed hundreds of thousands of
ineligible voters, people who have died or moved away.

North Carolina follows our voter roll clean-up successes in
California, Kentucky, Ohio, and Indiana. And we are right now prepping
lawsuits against other states to force them to clean up their rolls.

In November 2021, we sent letters
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26307&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctMTQtY291bnRpZXMtNS1zdGF0ZXMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
to election
officials in 14 counties and five states—Arkansas, California,
Illinois, New York, and Oregon—notifying them of evident violations
of the NVRA. The letters detail how these states’ own reported data
show that their counties removed an “absurdly low” or
“impossible” number of inactive voter registrations under key
provisions of the NVRA. The letters threaten federal lawsuits unless
the violations are corrected in a timely fashion.

So, in short, North Carolina is just the beginning ...

HILLARYGATE: TECHS ALLEGEDLY USED BY CLINTON TO SPY ON TRUMP WORKED
WITH DEFENSE AGENCY

We’re adding more fascinating detail to the growing pile of evidence
of Deep State collusion against President Trump.

The latest: Four people mentioned in the indictment of Michael
Sussmann by Special Counsel John Durham were paid for work with the
Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA) from 2016-2021 and
appeared to be interested in targeting Trump campaign adviser Steve
Bannon.

We learned this in 127 pages
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26778&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2dlb3JnaWEtdGVjaC1zdXNzbWFubi1ub3ZlbWJlci0yMDIxLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
of records of communications among the four that we received from the
Georgia Institute of Technology through an October 13, 2021, Georgia
Open Records Act request. The four individuals are Rodney Joffe

The indictment’s “Originator-1” is April Lorenzen, chief data
scientist at the information services firm Zetalytics. Her lawyer,
Michael J. Connolly, said she has “dedicated her life to the
critical work of thwarting dangerous cyberattacks on our country,”
adding: “Any suggestion that she engaged in wrongdoing is
unequivocally false.”

The indictment’s “Researcher-1” is another computer scientist at
Georgia Tech, Manos Antonakakis. “Researcher-2” is Mr. Dagon. And
“Tech Executive-1” is Mr. Joffe, who in 2013 received the F.B.I.

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26785&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYnVzaW5lc3N3aXJlLmNvbS9uZXdzL2hvbWUvMjAxMzEwMjkwMDU5NDgvZW4vTmV1c3RhciVFMiU4MCU5OXMtUm9kbmV5LUpvZmZlLVJlY2VpdmVzLVByZXN0aWdpb3VzLUZCSS1EaXJlY3RvciVFMiU4MCU5OXMtQXdhcmQ/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]

In a court filing
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26786&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3VzLXYtc3Vzc21hbm4tZmVicnVhcnktMjAyMi0wMDU4Mi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES CIA FOR RECORDS OF CONTACTS WITH INDICTED CLINTON
CAMPAIGN LAWYER MICHAEL SUSSMANN

We’ve been quite interested in the CIA’s role in the Deep
State’s efforts to take out President Trump. As you’ll recall, we
sued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26819&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctc3Vlcy1jaWEtbWlsbGV5LXRydW1wLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
in
December to find out what the heads of the CIA and Defense Department
were discussing about the president.

Now it’s being reported that the CIA was in contact with a disgraced
Clinton campaign attorney. And so we filed a FOIA suit against the CIA
for records of meetings and phone conversations between any CIA
personnel and former Clinton lawyer Michael Sussmann, who has been
charged in Special Counsel John Durham’s investigation with making a
false statement to a federal agent (_Judicial Watch v. Central
Intelligence Agency_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26813&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtY2lhLXN1c3NtYW5uLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMDQxMi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
(No. 1:22-cv-00412)).

We sued after the CIA failed to reply to an October 26, 2021, FOIA
request for:

All records regarding any meetings or telephonic conversations between
any official or employee of the Central Intelligence Agency and Mr.
Michael Sussmann (formerly an attorney with Perkins Coie) between
January 1, 2015 and the present. This request includes, but is not
limited to, all notes, transcripts, summaries, or other records
created in preparation for, during, or pursuant to the meetings or
conversations.
On February 11, Durham, who is investigating the origins of the
Trump-Russia investigation, filed a motion that focused on potential
conflicts of interest related to the representation of Sussmann, who
has been charged
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26814&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3Yvc2NvL3ByZXNzLXJlbGVhc2UvZmlsZS8xNDMzNTExL2Rvd25sb2FkP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
with
making a false statement to a federal agent. Sussmann has pleaded not
guilty.

According to a February 15, 2022, Fox News report
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26815&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZm94bmV3cy5jb20vcG9saXRpY3MvZHVyaGFtLXByb2JlLXRlY2gtZXhlY3V0aXZlLTEtc2hhcmVkLWFsbGVnYXRpb25zLXR5aW5nLXRydW1wLXJ1c3NpYS1jaWE/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=],
a spokesperson for the individual labeled in Durham’s latest filing
as “Tech Executive-1” has seemingly identified the CIA as the
“second” federal government agency to which Sussmann provided an
“updated set of allegations” to “establish ‘an inference’
and ‘narrative’” tying Donald Trump to Russia.

Durham writes in the latest filing
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26816&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3VzLXYtc3Vzc21hbm4tZmVicnVhcnktMjAyMi0wMDU4Mi9dOj91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==

[O]n February 9, 2017, [Sussman] provided an updated set of

government (“Agency-2”). The Government’s evidence at trial will

pertaining to Trump Tower, Donald Trump’s New York City apartment
building, the EOP, and the aforementioned healthcare provider. In his
meeting with Agency-2, the defendant provided data which he claimed
reflected purportedly suspicious DNS lookups by these entities of

mobile phone provider (“Russian Phone Provider-1”). The defendant
further claimed that these lookups demonstrated that Trump and/or his
associates were using supposedly rare, Russian-made wireless phones in

Counsel’s Office has identified no support for these allegations.
The CIA is in cover-up mode about its communications with the
lawyer implicated in a shady spy operation against President Trump.
What is the CIA hiding about its role in this plot against President
Trump?

POPPER TESTIFIES BEFORE FLORIDA HOUSE CONGRESSIONAL REDISTRICTING
SUBCOMMITTEE

Senior Judicial Watch Attorney Robert Popper testified this week
before the Florida House Congressional Redistricting Subcommittee
during consideration of “PCB CRS 22-01 – Establishing the
Congressional Districts of the State.”

Popper’s testimony focused on how Florida’s proposed 3rd
Congressional District would be subject to legal challenge as a racial
gerrymander if Florida’s state legislature approves the new map.

Popper’s testimony
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26820&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3JvYmVydC1wb3BwZXItdGVzdGltb255LWZsb3JpZGEtZ2VycnltYW5kZXJpbmctZmVicnVhcnktMjAyMi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
points out that the Supreme Court has held:

“[R]edistricting legislation that is so extremely irregular on its
face that it rationally can be viewed only as an effort to segregate
the races for purposes of voting, without regard for traditional
districting principles and without sufficiently compelling
justification” states a federal, constitutional claim under the
Equal Protection Clause.
Popper notes that District 3 is vulnerable to being legally
challenged:

Turning to Congressional District 3 in the proposed plan, I believe it
will be vulnerable to a serious—and probably a winning—Shaw-type
claim under the Fourteenth Amendment. I understand that there will be
little dispute that the district was drawn with its racial
characteristics as the predominant consideration. I also understand
that the shape of the district will be well-explained by the effort to
include African-American populations around Tallahassee and
Jacksonville. Moreover, the district clearly violates traditional
districting criteria. Its Popper-Polsby
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26809&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3BvbHNieS1wb3BwZXItOS15YWxlLWxhdy1wb2wtcmV2LTMwMS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
score is 10%, and its Reock score
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26810&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanN0b3Iub3JnL3N0YWJsZS8yMTA5MDQzP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
is 11%. These are very low
compactness scores for any U.S. congressional district, and in both
cases these are the lowest compactness scores in the State of Florida.
We are a national leader in voting integrity and voting rights. We
have assembled a team of highly experienced voting rights attorneys
who stopped discriminatory elections in Hawaii, and cleaned up voter
rolls in California, Ohio, Indiana, and Kentucky, among other
achievements
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=21208&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdGFnL252cmEvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz].

In December 2021, we filed a lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26523&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctc3Vlcy1tZC1nZXJyeW1hbmRlcmluZy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
on behalf
of 12 registered Maryland voters who object to Maryland’s 2021
congressional redistricting plan on the grounds that it diminishes
their rights to participate in elections for the U.S. Congress on an
equal basis with other Maryland voters, in violation of the Maryland
Constitution.

Robert Popper joined our legal team as a senior attorney in 2013.
Popper specializes in gerrymandering cases. In 1991, with Professor
Daniel Polsby, Popper wrote an article describing a mathematical way
to measure the geographic compactness of congressional districts. This
standard is now known as the “Polsby/Popper
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26812&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3BvbHNieS1wb3BwZXItOS15YWxlLWxhdy1wb2wtcmV2LTMwMS9d4oCdP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz
criterion and is one of the most widely used tests of district
compactness. In 1997, Popper brought a lawsuit that ultimately led to
New York’s 12th Congressional District being enjoined as an
unconstitutional racial gerrymander. In 2005, Popper joined the Voting
Section of the Civil Rights Division of the U.S. Department of
Justice, where he worked for eight years. In his time at DOJ, he
managed voting rights investigations, litigations, consent decrees,
and settlements in dozens of states.

CALIFORNIA ON SHAKY GROUND IN ENACTING GENDER QUOTAS FOR CORPORATE
BOARDS

It seems that California didn’t rely too heavily on science in
deciding that the state’s corporations needed a quota of women on
their boards. As you know, we sued to stop it.

This week we released 220 pages
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26774&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3RhZ3MvY2EtYm9hcmRzLTIwMjIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
of
trial testimony by our expert witness, Jonathan Klick
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26775&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubGF3LnVwZW5uLmVkdS9mYWN1bHR5L2prbGljay8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=],
Ph.D., J.D. Klick, an
expert in econometrics, statistics and corporate law, testified during
the 27-day trial that the studies on which California relies to prove
its case are deficient and unreliable.

The trial came to a close this week and we await a decision by the
Court.

Our expert’s testimony came on days 15 and 16 of the trial in
California Superior Court challenging the constitutionality of
California’s gender quotas for corporate boards of directors (_Robin
Crest et al. v. Alex Padilla_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=14906&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2NyZXN0LWV0LWFsLXYtcGFkaWxsYS13b21lbi1vbi1ib2FyZHMtY29tcGxhaW50LTI3NTYxLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
(No.19STCV27561)). We filed the lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=14941&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcHJlc3MtcmVsZWFzZXMvanVkaWNpYWwtd2F0Y2gtc3Vlcy1jYWxpZm9ybmlhLW92ZXItZ2VuZGVyLXF1b3RhLW1hbmRhdGUtZm9yLWNvcnBvcmF0ZS1ib2FyZHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
in Los Angeles County Superior Court in 2019 on behalf of California
taxpayers Robin Crest, Earl De Vries and Judy De Vries. Closing
arguments were held this week.

Our lawsuit challenges a 2018 law known as Senate Bill 826, which
requires every publicly held corporation headquartered in California
to have at least one director “who self-identifies her gender as a
woman” on its board of directors by December 31, 2019. The law also
requires corporations to have up to three such persons on their boards
by December 31, 2021, depending on the size of the board.

In our lawsuit, we argue
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26424&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanVkaWNpYWwtd2F0Y2gtc3Vlcy1jYWxpZm9ybmlhLW92ZXItZ2VuZGVyLXF1b3RhLW1hbmRhdGUtZm9yLWNvcnBvcmF0ZS1ib2FyZHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
that the quotas for women on corporate boards violate the Equal
Protection Clause of the California Constitution, among other
provisions.

At trial, government lawyers defending the quota have alleged that
gender quotas not only remedy discrimination but also improve overall
corporate performance.

Our expert analysis
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=25917&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2NyZXN0LXYtcGFkaWxsYS1tc2otMjc1NjEtcGdzLTc4LTEzNS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
rejects this: “the evidence offered for each of these points
(underrepresentation of women on boards, discrimination as the cause
of this underrepresentation, and that research shows a differential
benefit of appointing women, as opposed to men, in terms of firm
performance) is deficient and unreliable.”

Klick testified further in trial that:

[M]ost of the results [on corporate performance related to gender
composition of boards], including the one cited in SB-826, don’t
involve even regressions, much less more sophisticated designs. And
likewise, as with the earlier study, the Credit Suisse study, provides
no indication of statistical significance as between the differences.
During the trial, Klick was on the stand for two days and testified
exhaustively about the statistical techniques and types of scientific
investigations that would be required to show a causal relationship
between gender quotas and increased corporate performance. During his
testimony, Klick took the court through the “numerous independent
studies” cited in Senate Bill 826 and an accompanying legislative
report that allegedly demonstrate “publicly held companies perform
better when women serve on their boards of directors.” Klick
summarized
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26777&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2NyZXN0LXYtd2ViZXItZm9ybWVybHktcGFkaWxsYS10ZXN0aW1vbnktamFudWFyeS0xMi0yMDIyLTE5c3RjdjI3NTYxLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
for the court the deficiencies and unreliability of most studies:

[T]he general, overall statement I would like to make is the
literature generically on women and boards is relatively
unsophisticated. What I mean by that is the sort of natural
experiments that I was talking about, which again by the late 1990s
had become the standard in empirical policy analysis, empirical
inference of this sort is largely absent in the literature [on women
on boards of directors] as a whole.
Beyond generalizations, Klick also provided the court detailed
explanations
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=26776&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2NyZXN0LXYtd2ViZXItZm9ybWVybHktcGFkaWxsYS10ZXN0aW1vbnktamFudWFyeS0xMy0yMDIyLTE5c3RjdjI3NTYxLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
of why the numerous studies and analyses relied on by the California
legislature were deficient and unreliable. He also pointed out a
plethora of other studies contradicting the presuppositions behind
Bill 826, which the legislature simply ignored. In addition, Klick
testified that the conclusions of a comprehensive literature review he
performed in 2017, before this case arose, focusing on studies that
examined the relationship between gender quotas, corporate performance
and the beneficial effects of quotas on women in the workplace
generally:

Remember that correlation isn’t causation.

But there are things that we can do through regression techniques and
through natural experiments and more sophisticated designs that gets
us more confidence in determining whether or not a given correlation
is causation, and we’ve had those tools for at least 20, 25 years
now.

And they are the tools that modern social scientists and policy
analysts, financial people use every day, and it’s a reasonably
strong consensus about the value of these tools.

If we look at the literature that was used directly in support of 826,
those tools largely seem absent.... seem to have been ignored, or, at
least, unremarked on in the route to 826 and the advocacy for 826. On
the particular results that we think we can draw from the literature
... a representative picture of the literature ... is that it draws no
definitive conclusions.

[M]any of the studies lead to a statistically insignificant
relationship between board composition and various outcomes for firms.
Of the studies that don’t find a zero or statistically zero effect,
there are, largely speaking, as many studies that find positive
effects as find negative effects. And that’s even before we put any
filters on quality of the studies and things like that.

That’s what I found for sure in my literature review that I did
prior to this case.

***

I’m not the only one that’s done an academic literature review in
this area. There’s been a handful of them, and they uniformly have
come to the same conclusion.

Perhaps my favorite one, just because of the ... source of it is a
literature review written in 2014 in the _Delaware Journal of
Corporate Law_ by Deborah Rhode and Amanda Packel.... [T]he reason
this is so notable is Deborah Rhode, she’s deceased now ... was at
Yale, and later when she was at the Stanford Law School, she really
was a founding mother of feminist legal studies and those sorts of
things – but she also was a great academic and a very honest
academic. And in her literature review in 2014 she said, you know, as
much as people might want there to be a business case for diversity,
the current literature does not support it.

***

[T]hose are just two, my literature review and Deborah Rhode’s
literature review, and there are others that come to roughly the same
conclusion. I think that’s probably the most fair and honest reading
of this literature.
This historic trial shows a discriminatory gender quota mandate
that is blatantly unlawful and unconstitutional. After weeks of
taxpayer money being used to defend the law, we hope the trial court
will strike down these pernicious gender quotas.

This isn’t our only action in this area.

In September 2020, we also filed a taxpayer lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=20716&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcHJlc3MtcmVsZWFzZXMvanctc3Vlcy1jYWxpLXF1b3Rhcy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
to
prevent California from enforcing Assembly Bill 979, which requires
the same corporation subject to the gender-based quota also to satisfy
racial, ethnic, sexual preference and transgender status quotas by the
end of the 2021 calendar year.

In January 2021, we filed a public comment
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57942&L=21653&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2VjLmdvdi9jb21tZW50cy9zci1uYXNkYXEtMjAyMC0wODEvc3JuYXNkYXEyMDIwMDgxLTgxOTE0NDEtMjI3Mjc4LnBkZj91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
with the Securities and Exchange Commission in response to a proposed
rule change requiring race and gender quotas on the boards of
corporations listed on the Nasdaq exchange.

Federal and state constitutional and legal prohibitions banning
discrimination are under assault by the extremist Left and Judicial
Watch is front and center in court and in the public square defending
the rule of law against this pernicious effort.

Until next week...

clean_body: Judicial Watch VICTORY for Clean Elections! [INSIDE JW] Judicial Watch Victory: North Carolina Settles Voter Roll Lawsuit after State Removes over 430,000 Inactive Names from Rolls [==] In a big win for us and voters in North Carolina, we settled [=] our lawsuit against the state and two of its counties after they removed over 430,000 ineligible names from the voter rolls. We filed the lawsuit [] against the state, Mecklenburg County and Guilford County in April 2020 (Judicial Watch v. North Carolina and North Carolina State Board of Elections, et al. [=. 3:20-cv- 211)). In June 2019, the U.S. Election Assistance Commission (EAC) had released data [=] showing that voter registration rates in a significant proportion of North Carolina’s 100 counties were close to, at or above 100% of their age-eligible citizenry – statistics considered by the courts to be a strong indication that a jurisdiction is not taking the steps required by law to remove ineligible registrants. Our analysis also showed that at the time of the EAC report the entire State of North Carolina had a registration rate close to 100% of its age-eligible citizenry. On December 11, 2019, we sent notice-of-violation letters [==] to the state and its two counties, warning them that lawsuits would be filed if timely action were not taken to clean up the voter rolls. In our April 2020 complaint [==], we argued that North Carolina, Mecklenburg County, and Guilford County failed to make reasonable efforts to remove ineligible voters from their registration rolls as required by the federal National Voter Registration Act of 1993 (NVRA). The lawsuit also claimed that these jurisdictions violated the NVRA by failing to make available to us public records concerning efforts to comply with the law. On August 16, 2021, the EAC released its latest survey data [==] from the states. In the settlement, we told the court: [T]he total number of inactive registrations reported by North Carolina dropped from about 1.2 million in 2019, to about 765,000 in 2021 (a 36% drop). The statewide percentage of inactive registrations dropped from 17% in 2019, which the complaint alleged to be a national outlier, to 10% in 2021, which is close to the median state inactive rate. The number of registrations removed for failure to respond to an address confirmation notice and vote in two consecutive elections has increased, from about 220,000 for the period reported in 2019, to about 590,000 for the period reported in 2021 (a 168% increase). With respect to the two North Carolina counties, we told the court: Data for Mecklenburg County and Guilford County also showed improvement. From 2019 to 2021, the percentage of inactive registrations reported in Mecklenburg County dropped from 15.5% to 13%, and in Guilford County from 19% to 11%. The number of registrations removed for failure to respond to an address confirmation notice and vote in two elections increased during that same period, from roughly 21,000 to 51,000 in Mecklenburg County, and from 7,000 to 33,000 in Guilford County (a 142% and 372% increase, respectively). In light of Defendants’ substantial increases in removals of ineligible voters since this suit commenced, Plaintiff has determined in good faith that this legal action should not be pursued. In 2020, we also sued Pennsylvania [], and Colorado [] for failing to clean their voter rolls. This is a win for the voters of North Carolina – because clean voter rolls help pave the way to cleaner elections. After we filed our federal lawsuit, North Carolina removed hundreds of thousands of ineligible voters, people who have died or moved away. North Carolina follows our voter roll clean-up successes in California, Kentucky, Ohio, and Indiana. And we are right now prepping lawsuits against other states to force them to clean up their rolls. In November 2021, we sent letters [] to election officials in 14 counties and five states—Arkansas, California, Illinois, New York, and Oregon—notifying them of evident violations of the NVRA. The letters detail how these states’ own reported data show that their counties removed an “absurdly low” or “impossible” number of inactive voter registrations under key provisions of the NVRA. The letters threaten federal lawsuits unless the violations are corrected in a timely fashion. So, in short, North Carolina is just the beginning ... HILLARYGATE: TECHS ALLEGEDLY USED BY CLINTON TO SPY ON TRUMP WORKED WITH DEFENSE AGENCY We’re adding more fascinating detail to the growing pile of evidence of Deep State collusion against President Trump. The latest: Four people mentioned in the indictment of Michael Sussmann by Special Counsel John Durham were paid for work with the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA) from 2016-2021 and appeared to be interested in targeting Trump campaign adviser Steve Bannon. We learned this in 127 pages [==] of records of communications among the four that we received from the Georgia Institute of Technology through an October 13, 2021, Georgia Open Records Act request. The four individuals are Rodney Joffe [==], April Lorenzen [], David Dagon [==], and Manos Antonakakis [=]. According to The New York Times [= Mr. Durham used a 27-page indictment [=] to lay out a far more expansive tale, one in which four computer scientists who were not charged in the case ‘exploited’ their access to internet data to develop an explosive theory about cyberconnections in 2016 between Donald J. Trump’s company and a Kremlin-linked bank — a theory, he insinuated, they did not really believe. * The indictment’s “Originator-1” is April Lorenzen, chief data scientist at the information services firm Zetalytics. Her lawyer, Michael J. Connolly, said she has “dedicated her life to the critical work of thwarting dangerous cyberattacks on our country,” adding: “Any suggestion that she engaged in wrongdoing is unequivocally false.” The indictment’s “Researcher-1” is another computer scientist at Georgia Tech, Manos Antonakakis. “Researcher-2” is Mr. Dagon. And “Tech Executive-1” is Mr. Joffe, who in 2013 received the F.B.I. Director’s Award [=] for helping crack a cybercrime case, and retired this month from Neustar, another information services company. In a court filing [=] last week, Durham alleged this operation directly spied on Trump Tower, Trump’s home, and the Trump White House by exploiting “access to non-public and/or proprietary Internet data.” The anti-Trump operation used the “assistance of researchers at a U.S.-based university who were receiving and analyzing large amounts of Internet data in connection with a pending federal government cybersecurity research contract.” Here is some of what the four wrote to each other. On November 18, 2016, a redacted email [==] address writes on “behalf of Manos Antonakakis” to two Georgia Tech officials in an email titled “Signed DARPA Contract:” Hey Ashley, Please send to Michael the signed contract for their records. Thanks, Manos On November 21, 2016, Ashley Williams, a Georgia Tech contracting officer, replies: Good morning!! Attached is a copy of the new award for your records. Please note the contract is subject to publication restrictions identified in the DD 254. I'm actively working with AFRL [likely Air Force Research Laboratory] to revise the DD 254 to clarify that fundamental research is excluded from the publication restrictions. Although we’ve signed the contract award and I have to defer project initiation until the publication restrictions are resolved by the AFRL sponsor. Let me know if you have any questions. On August 2, 2016, Antonakakis writes [] to Danielle Gambino and Keromytis, Angelos, a DARPA employee: The subs and I, would like to have the permission to begin spending against the project from August 15th. This is the date when students needs to be hired [as graduate research assistants], so we can execute against the goals we have set in the SOW [statement of work] this year. UNC, GT and UGA would require an acknowledgment from you (or DARPA) that we are allowed to do that. I guess, once we are done negotiating the contract we will have to have as an effective start date the August 15th. If we cannot do that, it appears that it will complicate things for all three schools, as we cannot immediately hire the students necessary that will execute against the set milestones. Please let me know how you think we should resolve this issue. At 2:55 p.m. Angelos replies, “I'm ok with that, but I seriously doubt the contracting officer will agree.” At 3:16 p.m. Gambino also replies: As usual, Angelos is correct! Working with contracting to authorize pre-award work can take a while and typically is not allowed until closer to contract award. Although I certainly appreciate your eagerness to start working, we are at the very start of the contracting process - this is really way too early for this type of request. (The contract specialists haven't even been assigned yet.) Please don't hesitate to reach out to me with any other questions. On January 9, 2017, a DARPA employee, Kelly McLaughlin, follows up with Antonakakis. She writes: Manos, DARPA put $153,138 on the Georgia Tech Transparent Computing (TC) contract back in October, 2016 to cover the costs proposed in the attached SOW. The SOW asked for 0.83 month of your academic salary, salary for one Research Engineer, David Dagon, and funds for four graduate student research assistants. Were the proposed grad student costs supposed to cover UNC grad students or Georgia Tech grad students? The SOW shows them as Georgia Tech students. Please let me know if these funds were supposed to cover UNC. If so, the TC BFM, Laurisa Goergen, will reach out to the TC admin POC for Georgia Tech to see what, if anything, can be done at this point. Please let me know if you have any questions. Emails indicate that Neustar employees [] may have visited Georgia Tech to collaborate with Antonakakis. On May 27, 2016, Atreya Mohan from Neustar writes in an email with the subject line “Introducing Peter Burke:” Hello Manos Just wanted to introduce you to Peter (our SVP Engg and Operations). Peter. We contact Manos on his gmail account for consulting purposes and his Georgia Tech email address for interactions that relate with the university (example: sponsorship etc)” Burke replies: Hi Manos, It was great to meet you today – it is very interesting work that you do and I see great opportunities to collaborate with you. I would like to try to figure out a time to come and spend more time with so that you can continue my education :-) I am guessing you are based in Atlanta? Antonakakis writes that evening: Hey Mohan, Many thanks for the intro. Peter, the pleasure was all mine! Yes, you should come and visit us. I would suggest sometime in September, when the semester starts and my students are back from their internships in the bay area. You are more than welcome to visit sooner, but it will be just me and my three postdocs. The Neustar team is always welcome to visit my lab. Anytime you, Rodney, Brian or anyone else want to visit. Antonakakis responds to this chain again on July 14, 2016, writing: Gentlemen, By now all of you should be aware of the great news from DARPA. We have a 5 year long collaboration ahead of us, so I think it would make sense for the Neustar team to visit Atlanta and my lab. How is the week of August 15 looks like for you? Mine is completely open. Perhaps, we should schedule the visit then? The emails highlight that the ‘tech’ experts implicated in the Durham indictment were very much interested in the fake dossier used to smear President Trump. In an email [] to Antonakakis on January 29, 2017, Dagon writes: The Russians are killing spies with knowledge of the dossier materials: = Oh, and Trump purged the National Security Council (removing General Dunford) and put Steve Bannon (his PR guy) on the NSC: = My guess: The purged NSC will now say that Russia has given us great intel on ISIS, and that we should lift sanctions now that Russia is helping. (The public will have no way to judget [sic] this.) All this to protect Trump from the dossier materials. Antonakakis replies the same day, “What the [fck] is going on? Can you please explain why GOP is not doing something?” He then writes again a few minutes later: Some in the GOP knows what's up (Graham, McCain), but most are all too happy to have their narrow, specific agendas advanced (e.g., removing social security, ACA/Obama-care repeal, more tax cuts for companies, etc.) They put party ahead of country, in short. In 2018 the Senate (and maybe the House) may flip, and there will then be real investigations (but again, party will be ahead of country, as Democrats look into corruption for narrow purposes). Now that the Russians are killing people with knowledge of the dossier, we can hope for a defector who gets to a non-US embassy in Moscow. An August 25, 2016, email [==] from Joffe to Antonakakis, Dagon and Lorenzen indicates a possible interest in investigating Steve Bannon. The subject line is “To be added....” Joffe writes: “They think he may have some baggage... ;-)” A link to a Washington Post [=] opinion piece is included. Was the Defense Department’s DARPA funding and sensitive data misused by the Clinton campaign to spy on the Trump White House? If so, the criminal liability for those involved may be significant. JUDICIAL WATCH SUES CIA FOR RECORDS OF CONTACTS WITH INDICTED CLINTON CAMPAIGN LAWYER MICHAEL SUSSMANN We’ve been quite interested in the CIA’s role in the Deep State’s efforts to take out President Trump. As you’ll recall, we sued [==] in December to find out what the heads of the CIA and Defense Department were discussing about the president. Now it’s being reported that the CIA was in contact with a disgraced Clinton campaign attorney. And so we filed a FOIA suit against the CIA for records of meetings and phone conversations between any CIA personnel and former Clinton lawyer Michael Sussmann, who has been charged in Special Counsel John Durham’s investigation with making a false statement to a federal agent (Judicial Watch v. Central Intelligence Agency [=] (No. 1:22-cv-00412)). We sued after the CIA failed to reply to an October 26, 2021, FOIA request for: All records regarding any meetings or telephonic conversations between any official or employee of the Central Intelligence Agency and Mr. Michael Sussmann (formerly an attorney with Perkins Coie) between January 1, 2015 and the present. This request includes, but is not limited to, all notes, transcripts, summaries, or other records created in preparation for, during, or pursuant to the meetings or conversations. On February 11, Durham, who is investigating the origins of the Trump-Russia investigation, filed a motion that focused on potential conflicts of interest related to the representation of Sussmann, who has been charged [] with making a false statement to a federal agent. Sussmann has pleaded not guilty. According to a February 15, 2022, Fox News report [=], a spokesperson for the individual labeled in Durham’s latest filing as “Tech Executive-1” has seemingly identified the CIA as the “second” federal government agency to which Sussmann provided an “updated set of allegations” to “establish ‘an inference’ and ‘narrative’” tying Donald Trump to Russia. Durham writes in the latest filing [== [O]n February 9, 2017, [Sussman] provided an updated set of allegations – including the [Alfa Bank] data and additional allegations relating to Trump – to a second agency of the U.S. government (“Agency-2”). The Government’s evidence at trial will establish that these additional allegations relied, in part, on the purported DNS traffic that Tech Executive-1 and others had assembled pertaining to Trump Tower, Donald Trump’s New York City apartment building, the EOP, and the aforementioned healthcare provider. In his meeting with Agency-2, the defendant provided data which he claimed reflected purportedly suspicious DNS lookups by these entities of internet protocol (“IP”) addresses affiliated with a Russian mobile phone provider (“Russian Phone Provider-1”). The defendant further claimed that these lookups demonstrated that Trump and/or his associates were using supposedly rare, Russian-made wireless phones in the vicinity of the White House and other locations. The Special Counsel’s Office has identified no support for these allegations. The CIA is in cover-up mode about its communications with the lawyer implicated in a shady spy operation against President Trump. What is the CIA hiding about its role in this plot against President Trump? POPPER TESTIFIES BEFORE FLORIDA HOUSE CONGRESSIONAL REDISTRICTING SUBCOMMITTEE Senior Judicial Watch Attorney Robert Popper testified this week before the Florida House Congressional Redistricting Subcommittee during consideration of “PCB CRS 22-01 – Establishing the Congressional Districts of the State.” Popper’s testimony focused on how Florida’s proposed 3rd Congressional District would be subject to legal challenge as a racial gerrymander if Florida’s state legislature approves the new map. Popper’s testimony [=] points out that the Supreme Court has held: “[R]edistricting legislation that is so extremely irregular on its face that it rationally can be viewed only as an effort to segregate the races for purposes of voting, without regard for traditional districting principles and without sufficiently compelling justification” states a federal, constitutional claim under the Equal Protection Clause. Popper notes that District 3 is vulnerable to being legally challenged: Turning to Congressional District 3 in the proposed plan, I believe it will be vulnerable to a serious—and probably a winning—Shaw-type claim under the Fourteenth Amendment. I understand that there will be little dispute that the district was drawn with its racial characteristics as the predominant consideration. I also understand that the shape of the district will be well-explained by the effort to include African-American populations around Tallahassee and Jacksonville. Moreover, the district clearly violates traditional districting criteria. Its Popper-Polsby [=] score is 10%, and its Reock score [] is 11%. These are very low compactness scores for any U.S. congressional district, and in both cases these are the lowest compactness scores in the State of Florida. We are a national leader in voting integrity and voting rights. We have assembled a team of highly experienced voting rights attorneys who stopped discriminatory elections in Hawaii, and cleaned up voter rolls in California, Ohio, Indiana, and Kentucky, among other achievements []. In December 2021, we filed a lawsuit [=] on behalf of 12 registered Maryland voters who object to Maryland’s 2021 congressional redistricting plan on the grounds that it diminishes their rights to participate in elections for the U.S. Congress on an equal basis with other Maryland voters, in violation of the Maryland Constitution. Robert Popper joined our legal team as a senior attorney in 2013. Popper specializes in gerrymandering cases. In 1991, with Professor Daniel Polsby, Popper wrote an article describing a mathematical way to measure the geographic compactness of congressional districts. This standard is now known as the “Polsby/Popper [ criterion and is one of the most widely used tests of district compactness. In 1997, Popper brought a lawsuit that ultimately led to New York’s 12th Congressional District being enjoined as an unconstitutional racial gerrymander. In 2005, Popper joined the Voting Section of the Civil Rights Division of the U.S. Department of Justice, where he worked for eight years. In his time at DOJ, he managed voting rights investigations, litigations, consent decrees, and settlements in dozens of states. CALIFORNIA ON SHAKY GROUND IN ENACTING GENDER QUOTAS FOR CORPORATE BOARDS It seems that California didn’t rely too heavily on science in deciding that the state’s corporations needed a quota of women on their boards. As you know, we sued to stop it. This week we released 220 pages [] of trial testimony by our expert witness, Jonathan Klick [=], Ph.D., J.D. Klick, an expert in econometrics, statistics and corporate law, testified during the 27-day trial that the studies on which California relies to prove its case are deficient and unreliable. The trial came to a close this week and we await a decision by the Court. Our expert’s testimony came on days 15 and 16 of the trial in California Superior Court challenging the constitutionality of California’s gender quotas for corporate boards of directors (Robin Crest et al. v. Alex Padilla [==] (No.19STCV27561)). We filed the lawsuit [] in Los Angeles County Superior Court in 2019 on behalf of California taxpayers Robin Crest, Earl De Vries and Judy De Vries. Closing arguments were held this week. Our lawsuit challenges a 2018 law known as Senate Bill 826, which requires every publicly held corporation headquartered in California to have at least one director “who self-identifies her gender as a woman” on its board of directors by December 31, 2019. The law also requires corporations to have up to three such persons on their boards by December 31, 2021, depending on the size of the board. In our lawsuit, we argue [] that the quotas for women on corporate boards violate the Equal Protection Clause of the California Constitution, among other provisions. At trial, government lawyers defending the quota have alleged that gender quotas not only remedy discrimination but also improve overall corporate performance. Our expert analysis [=] rejects this: “the evidence offered for each of these points (underrepresentation of women on boards, discrimination as the cause of this underrepresentation, and that research shows a differential benefit of appointing women, as opposed to men, in terms of firm performance) is deficient and unreliable.” Klick testified further in trial that: [M]ost of the results [on corporate performance related to gender composition of boards], including the one cited in SB-826, don’t involve even regressions, much less more sophisticated designs. And likewise, as with the earlier study, the Credit Suisse study, provides no indication of statistical significance as between the differences. During the trial, Klick was on the stand for two days and testified exhaustively about the statistical techniques and types of scientific investigations that would be required to show a causal relationship between gender quotas and increased corporate performance. During his testimony, Klick took the court through the “numerous independent studies” cited in Senate Bill 826 and an accompanying legislative report that allegedly demonstrate “publicly held companies perform better when women serve on their boards of directors.” Klick summarized [==] for the court the deficiencies and unreliability of most studies: [T]he general, overall statement I would like to make is the literature generically on women and boards is relatively unsophisticated. What I mean by that is the sort of natural experiments that I was talking about, which again by the late 1990s had become the standard in empirical policy analysis, empirical inference of this sort is largely absent in the literature [on women on boards of directors] as a whole. Beyond generalizations, Klick also provided the court detailed explanations [==] of why the numerous studies and analyses relied on by the California legislature were deficient and unreliable. He also pointed out a plethora of other studies contradicting the presuppositions behind Bill 826, which the legislature simply ignored. In addition, Klick testified that the conclusions of a comprehensive literature review he performed in 2017, before this case arose, focusing on studies that examined the relationship between gender quotas, corporate performance and the beneficial effects of quotas on women in the workplace generally: Remember that correlation isn’t causation. But there are things that we can do through regression techniques and through natural experiments and more sophisticated designs that gets us more confidence in determining whether or not a given correlation is causation, and we’ve had those tools for at least 20, 25 years now. And they are the tools that modern social scientists and policy analysts, financial people use every day, and it’s a reasonably strong consensus about the value of these tools. If we look at the literature that was used directly in support of 826, those tools largely seem absent.... seem to have been ignored, or, at least, unremarked on in the route to 826 and the advocacy for 826. On the particular results that we think we can draw from the literature ... a representative picture of the literature ... is that it draws no definitive conclusions. [M]any of the studies lead to a statistically insignificant relationship between board composition and various outcomes for firms. Of the studies that don’t find a zero or statistically zero effect, there are, largely speaking, as many studies that find positive effects as find negative effects. And that’s even before we put any filters on quality of the studies and things like that. That’s what I found for sure in my literature review that I did prior to this case. * I’m not the only one that’s done an academic literature review in this area. There’s been a handful of them, and they uniformly have come to the same conclusion. Perhaps my favorite one, just because of the ... source of it is a literature review written in 2014 in the Delaware Journal of Corporate Law by Deborah Rhode and Amanda Packel.... [T]he reason this is so notable is Deborah Rhode, she’s deceased now ... was at Yale, and later when she was at the Stanford Law School, she really was a founding mother of feminist legal studies and those sorts of things – but she also was a great academic and a very honest academic. And in her literature review in 2014 she said, you know, as much as people might want there to be a business case for diversity, the current literature does not support it. * [T]hose are just two, my literature review and Deborah Rhode’s literature review, and there are others that come to roughly the same conclusion. I think that’s probably the most fair and honest reading of this literature. This historic trial shows a discriminatory gender quota mandate that is blatantly unlawful and unconstitutional. After weeks of taxpayer money being used to defend the law, we hope the trial court will strike down these pernicious gender quotas. This isn’t our only action in this area. In September 2020, we also filed a taxpayer lawsuit [=] to prevent California from enforcing Assembly Bill 979, which requires the same corporation subject to the gender-based quota also to satisfy racial, ethnic, sexual preference and transgender status quotas by the end of the 2021 calendar year. In January 2021, we filed a public comment [==] with the Securities and Exchange Commission in response to a proposed rule change requiring race and gender quotas on the boards of corporations listed on the Nasdaq exchange. Federal and state constitutional and legal prohibitions banning discrimination are under assault by the extremist Left and Judicial Watch is front and center in court and in the public square defending the rule of law against this pernicious effort. Until next week... Friend, I need your help.

Joe Biden is determined to DESTROY my Administration’s incredible America FIRST legacy.

name: Bryan Steil, Congressman
subject: Fighting Fentanyl Related Substances
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY STEIL FOR WISCONSIN, INC
body:
Peter, Fentanyl is destroying communities across our country. We must take action.
Peter,**64% of total United States overdose deaths are from fentanyl-related substances. We must take action.** That is why I held a roundtable with law enforcement officials in Southeast Wisconsin to hear firsthand how it is impacting our communities. **Fentanyl is being produced in China, shipped to Mexico, and then being trafficked across our southern border by drug cartels. Last fiscal year, 11,201 pounds of fentanyl were seized by Border Patrol. That is enough to kill every American 7 times. **_The Biden Administration 's failure to secure our border is destroying our communities. We must end the lawlessness at the border and give our border officials the resources they need to do their jobs._You can read more about my efforts to fight fentanyl at home by clicking the headlines below. [](https://email.rally.digital/t/j-l-zdrhrld-idtjvlhlh-r/) [](https://email.rally.digital/t/j-l-zdrhrld-idtjvlhlh-y/) We must secure our border, we must stop this poison from entering our country, and we must fund our law enforcement officers to keep our communities safe.Bryan
**--
Bryan Steil**
Congressman [](https://email.rally.digital/t/j-l-zdrhrld-idtjvlhlh-j/)[](https://email.rally.digital/t/j-l-zdrhrld-idtjvlhlh-t/)[](https://email.rally.digital/t/j-l-zdrhrld-idtjvlhlh-i/) [ Forward ](https://steilforwisconsininc.forwardtomyfriend.com/j-idtjvlhlh-C89B6A88-zdrhrld-l-h) | PAID FOR BY STEIL FOR WISCONSIN, INC
