clean_body: | Hi Peter, I've just published a new blog post: Two-thirds of swing voters say Democrats went "too far" in the COVID response. We are in for a reckoning of epic proportions. That reckoning just arrived for three school board members in San Francisco. It will next be visited upon the city’s district attorney, who faces his own Recall in June. Gavin Newsom, with his cratering poll numbers, can see the writing on the wall. As can our Legislature, apparently. The first shocking development is that to repeal Prop. 47 will get a vote in 2 weeks. The second is the Senate has finally agreed to debate cutting off Newsom's emergency powers. While we didn't end the emergency before the Super Bowl, we succeeded in shaming the Legislature into action. This comes as students across California walked out to protest the mask mandate. Our young people have been treated monstrously, but they’ve shown remarkable grit. That's one reason I believe we are lightning a new spark of liberty. The country is at a crossroads: follow California’s disastrous path, or restore America's Founding principles. Our battles against Newsom and the Supermajority have shown us just how much is at stake. Kevin Kiley California Legislator p.s. You can support the campaign by check . Paid for by Kevin Kiley for Congress 9458 Treelake Road, Granite Bay, CA 95746, United States

name: Beto HQ
subject: This isn’t the typical fundraising request
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for Texas
body:
Today, you have a chance to stake a special claim in our movement.
#
We’re so grateful that you’re part of the grassroots movement to elect Beto as the next Governor of Texas.**And today, you have a chance to stake a special claim in our movement.**
At midnight tonight, we’re up against another state-mandated reporting deadline. That means we are legally required to close the books on our fundraising and file a report with how much we’ve raised to the Texas Ethics Commission.
So far, Greg Abbott has outpaced Beto’s fundraising. Tonight’s deadline is a critical chance for us to show we’re capable of out-raising Abbott and his corporate donors through the power of grassroots donors.
What we’re asking for today isn’t the typical campaign fundraising request. You see, every donor who gives $90 or more will be publicly listed in our TEC report. So if you contribute $90 to this email, you’ll be on the record as a top supporter, and someone who made Beto’s historic victory possible.
**Will you take the next step and officially put your name on our Texas Ethics Commission report by donating to the link below?**
[**https://betofortexas.com/tec-donor?amounts=90**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/53265?amounts=90&name=&t=1&akid=176859%2E444211%2E1RUKfa)
To be clear, we couldn’t be more grateful for the simple fact that you’re with us. If today isn’t the right time to donate, don’t worry about it.
But if you are in a place to give, every name we add to our TEC report will boost our chances of winning in November and putting an end to Greg Abbott’s harmful governorship. Your continued support and belief in Beto — and in what we can achieve together — means everything.
Thank you for everything.
Team Beto

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bft-em-footer?refcode=em20220219-176859-footer&t=2&refcode2=176859_444211_1RUKfa&akid=176859%2E444211%2E1RUKfa)**

Our race to flip Nevada RED will determine the Senate majority, and our polling shows this TOSS-UP race will come down to just a handful of votes! The grassroots response to our campaign has been overwhelming and unprecedented. We have volunteers across Nevada reaching thousands of voters every day. But we’re quickly using up the supplies we need to keep our record number of volunteers in the field! In a race this close, we can’t afford to run short of these critical resources for even a single day. | Our volunteers are amazing, and they’re fired up to retake Nevada and the Republican Majority! name: Reeves Session Update
subject: Session Update!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY REEVES FOR CONGRESS
body:
Optional Masks, Service Weapons, VFRW, And More!
| | |
---
| | [](https://ltf91f0r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Fbrycereeves.com/1/0100017f1403903d-20446587-697c-4009-ba06-8e43a64ab0ce-000000/seqKERVnVS9C-iv7AkLicSvNhIQ=258)
---
| |
---
| **This Week in Session ** This past week officially marked the halfway point for the 2022 Legislative Session, having passed day 30 of our 60 day Session. The Senate is now reviewing House bills and visa-versa. I am very happy to report that my office got 12 bills through to the House, as we anticipated last week. Because of the difference in bill limitations and the amount of members between the House and Senate, we are going to start seeing longer sessions and committee meetings as we vote on legislation passed through the House of Delegates. One bill, that I co-patroned, has already passed through both the House and Senate, [SB 739.](https://ltf91f0r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Flis.virginia.gov%2Fcgi-bin%2Flegp604.exe%3F221%2Bsum%2BSB739/1/0100017f1403903d-20446587-697c-4009-ba06-8e43a64ab0ce-000000/cfpszJ6NSWPg6b2uFelXXzSVhds=258) I joined Governor Youngkin as he signed it into law on Wednesday. This new law requires schools to provide in-person education for all students as well as making it clear that parents, and only parents, get to decide what is best for their children when it comes to whether or not to wear a mask. I will always stand up for the rights of parents. That’s why as far back as 2013 I got [SB908 passed into law - codifying “that parents have a fundamental right to direct the upbringing, education, and care of their children.”](https://ltf91f0r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Flis.virginia.gov%2Fcgi-bin%2Flegp604.exe%3F131%2Bsum%2BSB908S/1/0100017f1403903d-20446587-697c-4009-ba06-8e43a64ab0ce-000000/aUnKAO2z3WQb6iUSFhkLqXHZ2t4=258)
---
| | |
---
| A multitude of my bills, as I mentioned earlier, made their way through to the House of Delegates including SB 768, which makes it easier for military families who have lost their loved one in the armed forces to have access to higher education. Multiple of my charitable gaming bills, which will help to clean up corruption in our Commonwealth’s charitable gaming system and bring our focus back to the charities themselves rather than the profits of large corporations, are awaiting House Committee assignments. SB 765, another bill I introduced that passed the Senate floor unanimously, would ease the process of allowing retired law-enforcement officers to purchase their own service weapon from their department. We had a wealth of friendly faces stop by our office this week, including a delegation from the Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office. Our friends in Taiwan are the 10th biggest trading partner to the USA as a whole, and have helped Virginia’s economy in a multitude of ways including the creation of 4,500 jobs in our Commonwealth. I also had a lovely time visiting with the Virginia Federation of Republican Women (VFRW) during their Legislative Day Breakfast. It was a pleasure chatting with activists from all over Virginia as they made their way to the Capitol to advocate for conservative. Our hallways were a sea of red on Wednesday, both from Valentine’s Day decorations and the bright red apparel of the VFRW.
---
| | |
---
| That’s all I have for you folks this week. If you have not had a chance to stop by the Pocahontas building for Valentine’s Day before, I highly recommend coming by next year. The staff in both the House and Senate put up festive decorations all through the halls. It truly is a sight to see, and our office was no exception with my Assistant Joy putting up all sorts of festive decorations for the enjoyment of members, staff, and visitors alike. Next week, the House and Senate will hash out their respective plans for our Commonwealth’s biennial budget. I will be sure to update you all in my next column. Thank you again for taking the time to read this, and for being active citizens that pay attention to state-level politics. Until next week,
---
| | |
---
| Bryce Reeves
---
|
---
| | PAID FOR BY REEVES FOR CONGRESS
---
|
---

| | | Please understand:

**Our Democratic governors are an ESSENTIAL firewall protecting the American people from radical Republicans and their fringe agenda. **

So if they LOSE in critical swing states, Democrats could be completely POWERLESS to stop Trump's GOP.

But the news isn't good: **All across the country, our most vulnerable Democratic governors are SINKING in the polls!!**

...We've **LOST** our lead in Michigan...
...We've **LOST** our lead in Wisconsin...
...And we're **TRAILING** the Republicans in Kansas!

****[At this pivotal moment, President Biden is IMPLORING grassroots Democrats everywhere to rise up and turn the tide before it's too late:](https://links.e.dccc.org/els/v2/vWPEQ9LR~)****

[](https://links.e.dccc.)

President Biden is absolutely right.

**And he knows the fight can 't end with our Democratic governors.**

Because without them, our historic House Majority becomes more important than EVER BEFORE...

...And as we write this, House Republicans are working non-stop to CRUSH our Majority once for all.

****[That's why we're RUSHING to answer President Biden's urgent call with a HISTORIC grassroots fundraising surge - 500 gifts before midnight - to defend our House Majority and hand Trump's Republicans a STUNNING rebuke.](https://links.e.dccc.org/els/v2/yr-)****

Please, can we count on you to answer the call with $3 to keep the dangerous GOP out of the Majority?

body:
I wanted to follow up with you myself.
| | | [](https://go.dscc.org/go/5250?ask=5&t=4&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20220219-Christie-SenateFocusGroupRef-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20220219-Christie-SenateFocusGroupRef-Pros-sl1&akid=3025%2E9716520%2E3BbQNo)

Hello -- Recent survey responses from you really stuck out to me, and I wanted to follow up myself.

As Executive Director of the DSCC, it’s my job to create a winning plan to help Democrats defend and expand our Senate majority -- and I need a few passionate supporters to help shape our strategy and ensure we don’t let Mitch McConnell take back the Senate.

**Input from this team made all the difference in the last election, so I'm kindly asking you to help us out again:**

**[Will you please enter our new _Senate Majority Focus Group_ to support Democrats as we prepare to defend our Senate majority and defeat more of Trump's Senate enablers?](https://go.dscc.org/go/5250?ask=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20220219-Christie-SenateFocusGroupRef-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20220219-Christie-SenateFocusGroupRef-Pros-sl1&akid=3025%2E9716520%2E3BbQNo)**

Folks,

Here’s the deal → A multi-million dollar super PAC funded by billionaire
Peter Thiel just spent $1.4 MILLION on one ad to “reintroduce” JD Vance to
Ohioans after his recent collapse in the polls.

Why this matters → If GOP mega-donors are spending $1.4 million on a
single ad just to prop up far-right extremist JD Vance, just IMAGINE the
money they will spend on baseless attack ads against Tim.

Our response → We need 297 donors to chip in right now to fight back
against the absurd amount of GOP dark money making this one of the most
expensive Senate races in the country.

name: Peter Welch
subject: What would affordable healthcare mean for you?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WELCH FOR VERMONT
body:
What would affordable healthcare mean for you and your family? Let us know:

# [](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/144?t=2&akid=110%2E61531%2EyCYaD1)
Hello,
Every day, people here in Vermont and across the country are forced to choose between paying a medical bill or paying for essentials. Parents put food on the table before they refill their prescriptions, teenagers ration their insulin for the month, people drive to the hospital for fear of ambulance bills. I hear these stories every day as Vermont’s Congressman, and I want to be very clear: Every American has a right to affordable healthcare, and it’s due time we delivered.
Americans everywhere are facing tough decisions regarding health insurance, prescription drug costs and difficulty paying medical bills. Premiums continue to rise, surprise bills still catch people trying to keep their costs low, and too many are forced to risk their health by staying uninsured.
For the past 15 years, I’ve fought to deliver relief for communities being affected by this. Medicare for All, lower prescription drug costs, and providing resources to those disproportionately affected could mean finally getting your teeth cleaned every six months — or affording your insulin without worrying if you’ll have to take that money from your rent.
[**I’ve spent my entire career fighting for Vermont by listening. That’s why I need to hear from you directly: what would affordable healthcare mean to you and your family? Click here and let us know.**](https://act.welchforvermont.com/signup/em-health-care-story-share?source=em_20220219_p_p_health&t=3&akid=110%2E61531%2EyCYaD1)
There are so many ways forward to ease the burden of the cost of care: we can remove barriers to the development of low cost generic drugs, let Medicare negotiate lower prices for prescriptions, , and pass Medicare for All. If we all come together to demand better from Congress, we can build the country we all deserve.
[**Take a moment to let us know what lowering the cost of healthcare would mean for you and your family.**](https://act.welchforvermont.com/signup/em-health-care-story-share?source=em_20220219_p_p_health&t=4&akid=110%2E61531%2EyCYaD1)
Thanks for sharing your story,
Peter

---
| [DONATE ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pwfv-em-22feb?refcode=em_20220219_p_p_healthcare&refcode2=110_61531_yCYaD1&akid=110%2E61531%2EyCYaD1)
